title: Disable SELinux labeling if privileged and user does not specify labels
body:
The previous patch mistakenly turned on SELinux even when --privileged.

This patch will disable SELinux, if the user specified --privileged and
did not specify any SELinux options.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

Hello,
I think this kind of revert to the previous situation where podman doesn't honour the "--security-opt" when "--privileged" is passed. Doing a test right now, and, well.... we're back to the previous state: "--privileged --security-opt label=filetype:container_share_t" doesn't apply the filetype (still container_file_t in the container).
(scratch my comment - there's another issue, --pid=host is preventing the application of --security-opt)
@cjeanner Please be more specific.  --pid=host will cause the SELinux labels to not be applied, is that the problem you are seeing?  What command exacly are you having problems with?

@rhatdan hello! so everything is explained here, with a reproducer: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1846364#c23
Lemme know if you need more infos.
